title: thymus lanceolatus subsp. numidicus
intro: Thymus lanceolatus subsp. numidicus, also known as Algerian thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant native to North Africa. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Algerian thyme include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce stress. Algerian thyme is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, essential oils, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and boost the immune system.
